text.title: Tools, Services, Agent Workflow, and Gotchas
text.summary: CLI tools, platform services, recommended agent workflow, and critical failure modes for multimodal VUI projects.

list.item: gcloud (Google Cloud SDK) — access Dialogflow CX, Speech-to-Text, Text-to-Speech APIs; cloud.google.com/sdk
aws CLI — Amazon Alexa Skills Kit, Polly TTS, Transcribe; aws.amazon.com/cli
az CLI — Azure Cognitive Services (Speech SDK, LUIS); learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure
ffmpeg — audio processing and format conversion for TTS output validation; ffmpeg.org
Dialogflow CX (Google SaaS) — industry standard for multimodal flows; supports rich responses (cards, chips); REST API agent-friendly
Amazon Alexa Skills Kit — multimodal templates for Echo Show; APL for visual layer; REST/SDK
Voiceflow (SaaS) — visual dialogue builder with REST API export; good for rapid prototyping
ElevenLabs (SaaS) — high-quality TTS; agent can generate voice previews for design review; REST API
Rasa (OSS) — self-hosted NLU + dialogue management; Python API

section.title: Agent Workflow

rule.statement: A Claude subagent can generate a multimodal dialogue script (voice_prompt, visual_state, user_actions, fallback) and validate it programmatically. Visual implementation (actual screen layouts, component assembly) requires human designer review.
rule.rationale: Agents cannot validate touch target sizing, card visual hierarchy, or readability at target screen size without a rendering environment.

section.reference: Agent prompt: generate multimodal dialogue script for a task and device

antipattern.description: Agent-generated dialogue defaults to formal register regardless of persona brief
antipattern.reason: Prompt explicitly for the target persona tone (e.g., "casual, helpful, under 12 words per turn"). Without explicit constraints, LLMs default to formal assistant register.

antipattern.description: Agent assumes instant LLM response with no latency design
antipattern.reason: LLM response latency in a live voice chain is user-visible (1-3s). Agents must design streaming visual feedback for every turn involving an LLM call.

antipattern.description: Agent generates a dialogue tree referencing visual card components without verifying they exist in the design system
antipattern.reason: Always cross-reference generated component types against the actual component library. Non-existent components produce broken screens.

antipattern.description: Agent assumes high ASR accuracy in dialogue design
antipattern.reason: ASR accuracy varies significantly by accent and environment. Designs must include disambiguation turns and robust "I didn't understand" fallbacks for all critical paths.
